arguments.command: which llvm-symbolizer addr2line llvm-addr2line 2>/dev/null; apt list --installed 2>/dev/null | grep -E 'llvm|binutils' | head -10

data.reasoningText:
The user wants to symbolicate the .NET runtime frames in this Android tombstone. The key library is `libmonosgen-2.0.so` with BuildId `1eb39fc72918c7c6c0c610b79eb3d3d47b2f81be`. The frames are at various PC offsets within that library.

To symbolicate these, I would need:
1. The debug symbols for this specific build of `libmonosgen-2.0.so` (matching the BuildId)
2. A tool like `llvm-symbolizer`, `addr2line`, or `ndk-stack` to resolve addresses to function names


3. Access to Microsoft's symbol server at `https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols`, where I can use the BuildId to look up the corresponding debug symbols for this .NET runtime build.

Let me check what symbolication tools I have available and attempt to download the symbols using the BuildId.

arguments.command: which llvm-symbolizer addr2line llvm-addr2line 2>/dev/null; apt list --installed 2>/dev/null | grep -E 'llvm|binutils' | head -10

data.reasoningText:
Good, I have `addr2line`. But I need the actual debug symbols for `libmonosgen-2.0.so` with BuildId `1eb39fc72918c7c6c0c610b79eb3d3d47b2f81be`.

Let me try to download them from Microsoft's symbol server. The format for ELF symbols on the Microsoft symbol server is:

For the binary itself:
`https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/libmonosgen-2.0.so/elf-buildid-{buildid}/libmonosgen-2.0.so`


For the debug file:
`https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/_.debug/elf-buildid-sym-{buildid}/_.debug`

I could try both URLs with the BuildId, or I could use the `dotnet-symbol` tool if it's available. Let me also consider whether `llvm-symbolizer` might be better for cross-architecture binaries, though I'm not sure if it would work better than `addr2line` in this case.

Actually, `addr2line` from x86 binutils won't work with arm64 binaries, so I need either an aarch64 cross-toolchain or architecture-independent LLVM tools. Let me check if `llvm-symbolizer` is available.

arguments.command: which llvm-symbolizer-20 llvm-symbolizer-19 llvm-symbolizer-18 llvm-objdump-20 2>/dev/null && llvm-symbolizer-20 --version 2>/dev/null | head -2

arguments.command:
# Try downloading the debug symbols from Microsoft symbol server
# BuildId: 1eb39fc72918c7c6c0c610b79eb3d3d47b2f81be
BUILDID="1eb39fc72918c7c6c0c610b79eb3d3d47b2f81be"
SYMDIR="/tmp/sv-314b0ac8b4274d55a2ae01e375cd13a5/symbols"
mkdir -p "$SYMDIR"

# Try the Microsoft symbol server with ELF buildid format
# Format: https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/<filename>/elf-buildid-<buildid>/<filename>
curl -sSL -o "$SYMDIR/libmonosgen-2.0.so" \
  "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/libmonosgen-2.0.so/elf-buildid-${BUILDID}/libmonosgen-2.0.so" \
  -w "HTTP %{http_code}\n" 2>&1 | tail -1

# Also try the debug info file
curl -sSL -o "$SYMDIR/libmonosgen-2.0.so.dbg" \
  "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/_.debug/elf-buildid-sym-${BUILDID}/_.debug" \
  -w "HTTP %{http_code}\n" 2>&1 | tail -1
arguments.description: Download debug symbols from MS symbol server
